title: SACRAMENTO

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Assembly Bill 3073 empowers the State Water Board to require local water agencies to collect and test wastewater samples for drugs like fentanyl, cocaine, methamphetamine, and xylazine.",
    "Wastewater-based drug testing has been successfully used in Europe for over a decade.",
    "Wastewater testing allows local governments to pinpoint dangerous spikes in illicit drug use at a neighborhood level.",
    "Wastewater drug testing can help local public health departments and law enforcement accurately allocate resources to tackle the rise of illegal drugs.",
    "The surge in illicit drug use has intensified California's struggle with overdose epidemics, with San Francisco's overdose-related deaths peaking at more than 750 in 2023.",
    "Wastewater-based drug testing is a proven technique for testing specific targets in drug use.",
    "The National Wastewater Surveillance System (NWSS) used wastewater testing technology to produce community-level data that helped limit the spread of COVID-19.",
    "Wastewater drug testing has been used for the past 20 years in Europe to identify drug use hotspots.",
    "The National Institute on Drug Abuse is using wastewater drug testing as part of a pilot program in over 70 cities nationwide.",
    "Testing wastewater for illicit drugs allows public health departments to identify trends in drug use and proactively target public health interventions."
  ]
}
```
